salary_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources); archivists £24-30k building, with experienced and senior archivists, and digital-records/information-governance roles £35-42k+ (management and specialist roles higher); modestly paid, with the digital and records-management corners better-paid and more in-demand
demand_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources)

salary_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources); agency assistants/junior agents start modestly (£22-30k), with established agents earning well via commission (typically ~10-20% of clients' earnings) — successful agents £50-100k+ and top agents with major clients far more; income is commission-and-roster-dependent, so it scales with your clients' success
demand_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources)

salary_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources); commercial photographers are largely self-employed, with income variable and skill/specialism-dependent — from building/part-time to £30-45k for established photographers and £50-55k+ for sought-after specialists (commercial, advertising, and niche experts more); the commodity end pays poorly, the specialist end well
demand_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources)

salary_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources); conference interpreters earn strongly — staff interpreters at international organisations are well-paid, and freelance conference interpreters command high day rates (£400-700+/day, more for rare/in-demand language combinations and AIIC-level interpreters), equating to £45-80k+ for established interpreters; among the best-paid interpreting work
demand_source: UK culture, knowledge, creative, and fashion sector data 2025/26 (CILIP, ARA, NRCPD/RBSLI, Prospects, sector salary data, fashion/media industry sources)
